Calibrate feature weights for an ELSA prioritization

Source: R/calibrate_weights.R
elsar_calibrate_weights.Rd

Iteratively computes a set of per-feature weights so that all features achieve more even representation relative to what each could attain on its own (its single-feature maximum). Under-represented features receive larger weights; well-represented features receive none. This is the pre-calibration step of the ELSA workflow: the resulting weights are multiplied by any user-supplied weights when the deployed tool solves the prioritization.

Usage

elsar_calibrate_weights(
  features,
  impacts,
  zones_pu,
  planning_units,
  budgets,
  lockins = NULL,
  feature_totals = NULL,
  wgt_scale = 2,
  gap = 0.05,
  solver = c("gurobi", "highs", "default"),
  threads = 4,
  time_limit = 3600,
  solve_retries = 2,
  progress_file = NULL,
  freeze_tol = 0.01,
  freeze_patience = 3,
  max_iter = 200,
  verbose = TRUE
)

Arguments

features

A SpatRaster of feature layers, ideally normalised to [0, 1]. Layer names are used as the feature identifiers throughout.

impacts

A data.frame mapping features to action zones. Must contain a feature column (matching names(features)) and one numeric column per action zone giving that feature's zone-contribution ("impact"), typically in [0, 1.5]. Zone column names must match names(zones_pu).

zones_pu

A named list of single-layer SpatRaster planning-unit masks (1 = available, NA = unavailable), one per action zone. If lock-ins are to be enforced, pass masks that already exclude lock-in cells from other zones. Names define the action zones and their order.

planning_units

A single-layer SpatRaster of all planning units in the study region (1/NA). Used as the objective budget and the denominator of area targets.

budgets

A named numeric vector of per-zone area caps, each a percentage of the study-area planning-unit count. Names must match names(zones_pu).

lockins

Optional named list of SpatRaster lock-in areas per action zone. Used only to raise a zone's target to its locked-in coverage when the lock-in exceeds the nominal budget (feasibility floor).

feature_totals

Optional named numeric vector of raw (non-impact-weighted) feature totals used as the representation denominator. Computed from features via terra::global(..., "sum") when NULL (the default).

wgt_scale

Maximum weight increment applied per iteration (default 2).

gap

Solver optimality-gap tolerance (default 0.05). Note: the ELSA max-utility problem is typically solved to an exact optimum at the root node, so this tolerance rarely binds.

solver

Solver backend: "gurobi" (default), "highs", or "default" (prioritizr's best available). "gurobi" requires the gurobi package.

threads

Number of solver threads (default 4).

time_limit

Per-solve time limit in seconds (default 3600).

solve_retries

Number of times to retry a failed solve (default 2), to survive transient solver or cloud-connection errors (e.g. a dropped connection to a Gurobi Compute Server). A short pause separates attempts.

progress_file

Optional path to a CSV. When set, each iteration's trajectory row is appended as it completes, so a long-running calibration can be monitored live (e.g. by a UI polling the file). The function is otherwise side-effect-free. Any existing file at this path is overwritten.

freeze_tol

Convergence threshold on the maximum per-feature change in representation between iterations (default 0.01). When the allocation stops changing by more than this, further additive reweighting cannot move the (exactly solved) optimum.

freeze_patience

Number of consecutive frozen iterations required to stop (default 3).

max_iter

Safety cap on the number of calibration iterations (default 200).

verbose

Logical; emit progress messages via log_message() (default TRUE).

Value

A named list of class elsar_calibration with elements:

weights

A tibble with columns feature and weight (the calibrated per-feature weight).

weight_matrix

The feature x zone weight matrix actually used (the do_nothing column is 0).

representation

A tibble with per-feature a (achieved representation), a_max, ratio = a / a_max, and delta_pct shortfall.

a_max

Named numeric vector of single-feature maximum representations.

trajectory

A tibble of per-iteration diagnostics (spread, max change, weight range, shortfall summary).

converged

Logical; TRUE if the loop stopped on the freeze rule rather than max_iter.

iterations

Number of calibration iterations run.

meta

A list of run metadata (solver, gap, wgt_scale, package versions).

Details

The routine reproduces the ELSA calibration framework exactly (it is not a new objective): (1) each feature's maximum attainable representation a_max is obtained by solving the zoned maximum-utility problem with that feature alone weighted; (2) the problem is solved with equal weights to obtain each feature's baseline representation and its relative shortfall delta = (a - a_max) / a_max * 100; (3) weights are adjusted iteratively with the additive, increase-only rule adj = wgt_scale - (delta - min(delta)) / (max(delta) - min(delta)) * wgt_scale, re-solving and recomputing shortfalls, until the allocation stops changing. The weight set giving the most even representation (smallest spread of delta) is returned.

Requires the prioritizr package (and a supported solver, e.g. gurobi or highs); both are optional dependencies.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
cal <- elsar_calibrate_weights(
  features       = feature_stack,
  impacts        = impact_df, # cols: feature, protect, restore, manage
  zones_pu       = list(protect = pu_protect, restore = pu_restore, manage = pu_manage),
  planning_units = pu_all,
  budgets        = c(protect = 30, restore = 18.564, manage = 5),
  solver         = "gurobi"
)
cal$weights
} # }
